etymology.description: semantic 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass; plant") + phonetic 波(波) (bō) -> leafy vegetable
etymology.interpretation: The character 菠(bō, 'spinach; leafy vegetable') combines the semantic component 艹(艹) (cǎo, 'grass; plant'), indicating a connection to vegetation, with the phonetic component 波(波) (bō), which provides the pronunciation. This combination denotes a type of leafy plant, specifically referencing spinach.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢吃炒菠(他喜歡吃炒菠)。
examples.english: He likes to eat stir-fried spinach.

examples.chinese: 我在市场上买了新鲜的菠(我在市場上買了新鮮的菠)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài shìchǎng shàng mǎi le xīnxiān de bō.
examples.english: I bought fresh spinach at the market.

examples.chinese: 菠汤最适合在寒冷的冬天喝(菠湯最適合在寒冷的冬天喝)。
examples.pinyin: Bō tāng zuì shìhé zài hánlěng de dōngtiān hē.
examples.english: Spinach soup is best enjoyed in the cold winter.
